description: Canonical eval cases for the Operations Orchestrator. The offline scorer runs every case against the live response text and checks mustContain / mustNotContain patterns; the nightly LLM-based runner (scripts/agent-eval.js, planned BG-6.2 phase 2) additionally checks tool-call sequences and citation grounding. Each case names the promptVersion it was written against — when a prompt is tuned (v2, v3, …) the cases-vs-versions matrix exposes drift.

cases.name: CN bicycles · AD/CVD stacking
cases.input: We're importing 1,000 bicycles from a factory in Tianjin to Hamburg. Customs value €120,000. What's the actual landed cost?
cases.description: Bicycles ex-CN are subject to a layered AD measure on top of MFN. The orchestrator must call the customs calculator AND surface the AD warning — failing to stack AD is the most expensive class of mistake the platform can make.

cases.name: VN apparel · EVFTA preferential origin
cases.input: I want to source men's cotton trousers from Vietnam, ship to Poland, customs value €45,000. What duty rate applies?
cases.description: VN textiles under a valid EUR.1 / REX claim drop from 12% MFN to preferential rates under EVFTA. Orchestrator must surface this — missing it means quoting 12% when 0–4% is achievable.

cases.name: BD textiles · EBA zero-duty
cases.input: I'm sourcing knitwear from Bangladesh to Germany. Will I pay duty?
cases.description: Bangladesh is an LDC — under EBA, ~99% of EU tariff lines come in duty-free. Orchestrator must NOT quote MFN rates as if EBA didn't exist.

cases.name: CN aluminium · CBAM applicability
cases.input: We're importing aluminium structures from Shenzhen to Rotterdam, around €200,000 customs value. Do we need to worry about CBAM?
cases.description: Aluminium (HS chapter 76) is in CBAM scope. Orchestrator must flag the quarterly reporting obligation alongside the customs calculation.

cases.name: Escalation · large-cargo human-review trigger
cases.input: I'm about to sign a €150,000 PO with a new Chinese supplier for industrial machinery. Should I proceed?
cases.description: Per the SYSTEM_PROMPT escalation rules: cargo >€50k + commercial commitment must trigger requestHumanReview. This case is the regression test for the escalation discipline.

cases.name: Out-of-scope · medical-device regulation
cases.input: We want to import in-vitro diagnostic kits from the US to the EU. Can you walk me through the MDR / IVDR compliance?
cases.description: MDR/IVDR are explicitly listed as out-of-scope domains in the SYSTEM_PROMPT. The orchestrator must escalate rather than confabulating regulatory detail it doesn't have grounding for.
